rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture and insert annex content only within a valid owner scope.
Keep component-type contract content in component type scope; keep implementation-level bindings only in implementation scope.
Use the input AADL model as the authority for owner names, features, subcomponents, connections, and scope.
generic_rules: Do not move or alter unrelated structural elements when fusing generated content.
Ensure referenced symbols are valid for the chosen owner scope before insertion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Subcomponent paths are implementation-scope references and should not appear in a component-type contract unless exposed through valid features or variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place feature-based assumptions or guarantees in an implementation annex when they belong to the component type.
Do not infer scope from requirement text alone; verify against the model structure.
irrelevant_topics: Numeric range expression patterns for clause generation
AADL property values as AGREE expression variables
